concerns.text: T044 (Baseline Correction Simulation) is scheduled AFTER T037 (Limitations Report) and T045 (Update Limitations). However, T045 explicitly depends on the findings from T044 ('Update... to include the findings from the Baseline Correction Simulation'). The current order places the consumer (T045) before the producer (T044) within the same phase block, violating the producer-before-consumer dependency rule.

T018 (ERA5 Fetching) and T019 (Geospatial Matching) are marked [P] (parallel-safe). However, T019 logic ('link Moral Machine records to nearest ERA5 grid') semantically requires the data fetched in T018 to be available in memory or on disk. Parallel execution of T018 and T019 would cause T019 to fail or race on missing data. These tasks must be sequential, not parallel.

T020 (Time-based interpolation) is marked [P] and listed after T019. Interpolation logic requires the matched dataset (output of T019) to identify gaps. Executing T020 in parallel with T019 is impossible as the input artifact (matched records with timestamps) does not exist yet. This is a hidden ordering violation.

T037 is listed under 'Phase 6: Limitations & Reporting' but is tagged [US3]. While US3 is Phase 5, the task description ('Update... to generate a final limitations report') implies it consumes the output of the entire analysis pipeline (US1, US2, US3). The grouping suggests it belongs to the final reporting phase, but the [US3] tag might confuse the dependency graph if the engine assumes US3 tasks only depend on US2. Clarify that T037 depends on the completion of Phase 5, not just the implementation of US3 code.
